events.title: 🌈 SIX The Musical
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
It’s time to take back the microphone and reclaim your crown!

We are SO excited to give away 4 tickets to the global sensation SIX for the opening night performance on Tuesday, May 5th, at the Tanger Center!

From Tudor Queens to Pop Icons, the SIX wives of Henry VIII remix five hundred years of historical heartbreak into a euphoric celebration of 21st-century power. The New York Times says SIX “TOTALLY RULES!” and The Washington Post hails it as “exactly the kind of energizing, inspirational illumination this town aches for!"

To enter: Tell us about a moment you felt fierce, stood up for yourself, or took back your narrative. We want to hear those "reclaim your crown" moments!

Two winners will be chosen to snag 2 tickets each for the May 5th show.

Show Details: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts Tuesday, May 5th (Show runs May 5-10).

 If you can't make the opening night, you can get tickets from the link in the comments.

We can’t wait to read your stories below. Let’s show them how Greensboro shines!

#SIXTheMusical #GreensboroPride #IMPAQTGSO #TangerCenter #ReclaimYourCrown

events.title: 🌈 Sunday Walk
events.location: Guilford Courthouse National Military Park Visitor Center, 2332 New Garden Rd, Greensboro
events.description:
New Time for Sunday Walk: 9 AM (Summer Hours)

*New Location:**
Guilford Courthouse National Military Park Visitor Center
2332 New Garden Rd, Greensboro

Join us at **9 AM** for a refreshing experience in nature filled with vibrant vibes! Come along with the Carolina Aging Alliance for a delightful stroll along the 2.5-mile path, where you can chat and connect with your neighbors.

To find us, please enter through the New Garden Road entrance. If the Visitor Parking Lot is full, no worries! Simply follow the road to park in one of the Auxiliary Lots or across the street in the Gravel Parking Lot.

Let's gather at the Visitor Center and enjoy a leisurely walk through the park together!

#excercise  #Inclusivity #CAA #IMPAQTGSO #community

events.title: 🌈 Lesbian Visibility Day
events.description:
🧡🤍💖 Today we celebrate Lesbian Visibility Day!!!🎉🎉🎉

A moment to honor the strength, love, resilience, and contributions of lesbian women everywhere. Visibility isn’t just about being seen, it’s about being recognized, valued, and uplifted in every space we enter.

As trailblazer Sally Ride once said:
“You can’t be what you can’t see.”

That truth still resonates. Visibility creates possibility. It opens doors for the next generation to live boldly, authentically, and without apology.

Today, we celebrate:
🧡 The stories that have been told, and the ones still waiting to be heard
🤍 The courage it takes to live openly and honestly
💖 The community that continues to build spaces of love, safety, and empowerment

To every lesbian who has ever questioned if they belong, you do. Your existence matters. Your love matters. Your visibility matters.

Let’s continue to show up, speak out, and stand proud, not just today, but every day.

#LesbianVisibilityDay #VisibilityMatters #Pride365 #GSOPride

events.title: 🎨 Public Open Studio Hours
events.description:
✨Summer Studios: Arts on Site! ✨
The Annual GPS Student Residency Program

We are thrilled to introduce you to the final Artist in Residence of our 2026 Summer Studios Cohort 🤘🏼Meet Macy James macyjart — Learn more about her and give her a follow, and keep swiping ➡️ Save those Dates for next week’s Open Public Studio Hours - see you there! 💪🏼

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work. The residency’s mission is to be an adaptive space to build a sustaining art community for our UNCG students so their creativity and connection to the Greensboro community thrives.

The students selected represent those who best considered how to use this residency as a way to work towards new or expanded approaches in their making practices. 

Mark your calendars and joins our AIRs in their studio practices 🗓️
Session #1: May 11-22, 2024

Public Open Studio Hours:
Monday, May 18th | 12-3P
Wednesday, May 20th | 2-5P

Closing Reception + Open Studio: 
Friday, May 22 | 6-8P

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: 🎨 4th Annual BA Student Exhibition
events.description:
A closer look into the 4th Annual BA Student Exhibition ✨🎨

Shoutout to our amazing BA student artists and the interns who helped curate this show.

*********************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
4th Annual BA Student Exhibition
Curated by GPS Interns:
Naomi Chavez, Kate Currivan, Melanie St. Clair
Exhibition: May 5-9, 2026
Reception: Thursday, May 7th, 6-8p
Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Thursday 12-5p
Friday, CLOSED Saturday, 2-5p
This exhibition marks the fourth annual Bachelor of Arts student exhibition hosted by Greensboro Project Space. Students are given the opportunity to display the skills that they have built during their time as an undergraduate student at UNC Greensboro.
Greensboro Project Space is proud to showcase BA students’ ambition, drive, and creative power to our community. These students have formed their own journey on their own terms; exploring painting, drawing, printmaking, sculpture, animation, and new media. We invite you to experience and celebrate their creative development throughout their academic journey.

events.title: 🎨 Homebody
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of Homebody by Kathryn Hood!🥂

The exhibition is still open until 5pm — don’t miss out on experiencing Homebody

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Homebody

MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Kathryn Hood

Exhibition: April 28 - May 2, 2026
Reception: Friday, May 1st, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Homebody is an exhibition of collages and sculptures in which Kathryn Hood carves her perceptions of tense muscles into paper and upholstery foam. She embraces the body-mind connection, an essential tool for self-understanding, as she houses ungrounded avatars within handmade paper and muscular furnishings outside of formal traditions. Hood composes the interior feelings of ideas & emotions with the exterior feeling of tension. This duality of perception reflects the disparate nature of themes explored, such as femininity, domesticity, and the performance of identity.

Homebody is the residence of textured perceptions. This space is an act of resensitization.

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: 🎨 Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us in celebrating the reception Garden of Corporeal Fantasy by Victoria Mercado-Lues

The exhibition is still open until 5pm — step inside before it closes

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Victoria Mercado-Lues

Exhibition: April 21 - 25, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 24th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Brightly saturated and fantastical, Garden of Corporeal Fantasy explores the meeting of nature and flesh in oil paintings and ceramics. With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder — a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful.

This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal.
Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

uncg uncgarts uncgartschool cvpastudents

With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder — a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful. 
 
This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal. 
Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile 

uncg uncgarts uncgartschool cvpastudents

events.title: 🎨 Love would feel like this Reception
events.description:
Enter Love Would Feel Like This. The space unfolds with every step.🍂🐚

Join us TONIGHT from 6-8pm to celebrate the reception of Love would feel like this by Inga Manticas 🥂

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS

Love would feel like this
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Inga Manticas

Exhibition: April 14 -18, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 17th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours: 
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

If I had one wish …

Love would feel like this consists of drawings and sculptures that grew up together in my studio. It’s a forest, a river of new substances, a procession of characters with disputed and shifting identities, material marriages, corkboards of a conspiracy theorist, and whatever else might be attracted to this place. 

I am trying to communicate with beings whose spirits and material forms are very different from my own. Beings who we can feel but cannot see or name, who exist two places at once, who have hundreds of identities, who are the opposites of themselves, who contain all the rest of us. What practices and processes can put me in touch with plants, land spirits, gods, animals, hills, rivers, gems, gnomes, ghosts, past or lost selves, the numinous and ineffable? 

I wonder what I can say to them and what I can give them, and I try to hear their responses. I wonder, how can I love them? How can I feel their love back? 

Learn more by clicking “Happening Now” in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: 🎨 Gallery Talks Galore
events.description:
📣Calling all Art History majors @ UNCG‼️ THIS CALL IS FOR YOU 🫵🏼 GPS is hosting a pop-up to go alongside the Annual Spring 2026 BA Student Exhibition to highlight the research done by art history BAs who might not have physical artwork to share with our community! We are inviting you to share a the research you have done during your time at UNCG and give a short gallery talk about your findings.

🔗 to submit your research proposal and participate is in BIO ✌🏼😎 …. More info below:

“Gallery Talks Galore” is an opportunity for students to share their research done during their time at UNCG with fellow art appreciators by giving a mini gallery talk. Presenters are asked to prepare 8-10 minutes of talking following with a short discussion among the audience. This is a casual, informal presentation that can be done by all levels of BA Students (First-year to Senior).

Senior BA students are especially encouraged to participate! 

Gallery Talks Galore! will be hosted on Thursday, May 7th from 5pm - 6pm at Greensboro Project Space, right before the exhibition’s celebratory Reception (6-8p); those participating in the pop up are encouraged to stay and enjoy free food and drink after their mini gallery talk/research presentation! 

This Pop-Up is a part of the public programming for the Annual BA art exhibition, which will run from May 5th - May 9th, 2026 at Greensboro Project Space that will sowcase artwork of BA Studio Arts majors.

events.title: 🎨 Death Circle
events.description:
𝔇𝔢𝔞𝔱𝔥 ℭ𝔦𝔯𝔠𝔩𝔢, 𝔱𝔬𝔡𝔞𝔶 𝔣𝔯𝔬𝔪 5 𝔱𝔬 7 𝔓𝔐 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs 🖤

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We’ll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there — 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: 🎨 advance_base & moontypo with likewindowpanes
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th 👾🎶🎹

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. We’re super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with Greensboro’s own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as “lo-fi,” “depressed” & “weirdly uplifting.” Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthy’s dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

Flyer by: likewindowpanes

events.title: 🎨 Maps, Luke Schneider & Saapato
events.description:
𝗝𝗼𝗶𝗻 𝘂𝘀 𝗼𝗻 𝗧𝗵𝘂𝗿𝘀𝗱𝗮𝘆, 𝗙𝗲𝗯𝗿𝘂𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝟭𝟵𝘁𝗵, 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮 𝗻𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 𝗼𝗳 𝗰𝗼𝘀𝗺𝗶𝗰 𝘀𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱 𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗹𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 🔊🪐🎵🐴

𝙈𝙖𝙥𝙨 features Brian Haran (Ama Divers, Filthybirds; engineer on numerous records) handling electronics/oscillators/tapeloops, Nick Vanbuskirk on sax (Panes, Kill the Buddha, bedroom division, and countless others), along with Tyler Monroe behind the drums (plays with too many people to count). This project is 100% improv—noisy ambient colliding with avant jazz. brian.s.haran likewindowpanes _tyler.monroe_ 

𝙇𝙪𝙠𝙚 𝙎𝙘𝙝𝙣𝙚𝙞𝙙𝙚𝙧 is a Nashville based pedal steel guitarist who has worked with a variety of artists including Margo Price, Orville Peck, S.G. Goodman, William Tyler, and Futurebirds. His debut solo album of ambient New Age music created entirely on pedal steel, ‘Altar of Harmony,’ was released by Third Man Records in 2020. His new EP ‘For Dancing In Quiet Light’ is out now via Leaving Records. lukefromnashville 

𝙎𝙖𝙖𝙥𝙖𝙩𝙤 is the music project of upstate New York–based sound artist Brendan Principato, who weaves manipulated field recordings with lush electronic soundscapes exploring ecology and music. He has held residencies with the National and Alaska State Park Services, including 2023 near Juneau and 2022 on Fire Island. His recordings have been named Best Ambient on Bandcamp, and his live performances include collaborations with Laraaji, Don Slepian, Patricia Wolf, and Green-House. saapato 

See ya swimming in the Pisces thresholds soon 🎛️🌀🌊🐚🌌🌒🐟

events.title: 🎨 jacobcmckelvie, kill_thebuddha & televoltmusic
events.description:
Join us on Sunday, October 5th for a sweet lil show! 🐌🐌🐌

Doors at at 7/music at 8/$10 suggested 

We’re welcoming jacobcmckelvie on tour from Massachusetts, alongside Greensboro’s own kill_thebuddha & televoltmusic 

jacobcmckelvie has traveled the DIY touring circuit in the US and UK for over a decade, carving out a niche for himself as a songwriter and performer. Known for his off-kilter and whimsical “stream of self-consciousness” lyricism and charming stage demeanor, he keeps audiences engaged both as a solo performer or with his newly minted backing band, “the New Old Band”. 

He released his latest LP “A New Kind of Hat” in 2024 and has been touring in support of that since.

Jake’s music has been featured on Americana UK, WFMU, Daytrotter, No Depression and has opened for acts like AJJ, Jeffrey Lewis, Clem Snide, Jeff Rosenstock and Steve Forbert.

“If you like a literate singer-songwriter with a witty turn of phrase then Jake is your man.” - Americana UK

“It just slyly worms its way into your brain.” - No Depression

See ya at the show! 🧃

events.title: 🎨 crisis.actress_ & officialclairemusic
events.description:
⛓️FRIDAY, OCTOBER 3rd⛓️DOORS at 8/SHOW at 9pm/$12 suggested/See ya at the show! 🤠

Chicago-based crisis.actress_ crafts experimental pop that exists in perpetual evolution. At the heart of their sound are swirling keyboards, layered vocal harmonies, loops, and motorik guitar lines that propel their performances into unexplored territories. Guests are frequent, though the core ensemble includes Brooks Hannon on guitar and electronics, Max Hudson on synthesizers and vocals, Sam Pardo on bass and vocals, and Jake Keenum on drums. Their full-length debut, Return of the Puppet King, captures the band’s shared musical obsessions and commitment to experimentation. Recorded at the hatlowed Electrical Audio studio last fall, each track serves as a testament to their collective journey a dream realized through collaboration with countless talented friends who helped shape their vision.

officialclairemusic is one of Chicago’s finest new songwriters. She works with calm confession, deep wit, and the details of a poet– all on top of big, swaying hooks. The Chicago Reader praised her “incisive, witty lyrics… a combination of sensitivity and straight talk.” She’s also a calm shredder of guitar– both gritty and delicate. Claire grew up in the Chicagoland area. Her music caught the attention of former Wilco member, LeRoy Bach. Bach is a member of her live band, (as well as Brooks Hannon & Jake Keenum from Crisis Actress.) Official Claire’s most recent studio offering, Midnight Dickbiter,features five remarkable new compositions.

patoiscounselors : Based in Charlotte, NC. Friends and fans of music making more music.
We’ve performed with Protomartyr, Gustaf, Ceremony, Automatic, Young Widows, BODEGA, Ekko Astral, Public Circuit, Truth Club, Quintron, The Messthetics, Omni, Sheer Mag, Ibex Clone, Algiers.

mostly_mesh : Bass heavy post-punk darkwave with campy references to 80’s horror, BDSM tinged lyrics, and the processing of trauma. Macabre love songs drenched in lust, sarcasm, and corn syrup blood. Mostly Mesh invites you to don your black lipstick and make out while a slasher plays in the background.

events.title: 🎨 DAGGERTEETH
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of hands—ink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

events.title: 🎨 LEAF LITTER
events.description:
We’re thrilled to spotlight a truly captivating series in our current exhibition, LEAF LITTER—‘Abalone Variation 1–6’ by the talented artist KT Wilson. These original works are a celebration of the subtle, often overlooked wonders of nature, and we invite you to experience them in person at Irving Park Art & Frame.
Each piece in this series features a sustainably sourced abalone shell (pāua) from the pristine waters of New Zealand. Revered for their iridescent beauty and cultural significance, New Zealand abalone shells are harvested under one of the world’s most robust sustainability systems, ensuring that their natural populations are protected for generations to come. These shells, considered ‘taonga’ (treasures) in Māori culture, are not only a feast for the eyes but also a testament to the harmony of art and ecological responsibility⁠.
KT Wilson’s approach transforms each shell into a meditative focal point by mounting them in custom shadowbox frames. Rather than traditional fastenings, Wilson uses carefully woven textiles and thread to cradle each shell in place. This technique both secures the shell and highlights its natural form, color, and texture, drawing attention to the unique qualities of each specimen. The interplay between the organic shell and the geometric textile work invites viewers to contemplate the contrast and connection between the natural world and human creativity.
The ‘Abalone Variation’ series is a perfect fit for LEAF LITTER, our exhibition dedicated to the nuanced, often unnoticed aspects of nature. This show brings together artists whose work delves into the delicate, the intricate, and the ephemeral—reminding us that beauty is often found in quiet details. KT Wilson’s pieces exemplify this ethos, offering viewers a chance to slow down and appreciate the subtle complexity of natural materials and thoughtful craft.
.
Visit us at Irving Park Art & Frame to see KT Wilson’s work and explore the full LEAF LITTER exhibition. Support local art, sustainable materials, and the ongoing conversation between human hands and the natural world.
.
.
.
#ArtExhibition #ContemporaryArt #NatureInspired #KTWilson #AbaloneArt #SustainableArt #PauaShel

events.title: 🎨 H2HMRKT : HOLIDAY 2025
events.location: ONE REP 207 S Westgate Dr STE A
events.description:
FLASHBACK FRIDAY ✨ Did you know that the first ever GSOZF was actually an extension of handtohandmarket and started in 2016?  The H2HMRKT is how I started producing events and it began in 2011.  It was the largest indie craft fair in Greensboro up until 2020 when the pandemic happened. Slowly but surely I have been coming back to event production and last year was the start of this when GSOZF came back. Now, I’m ready to bring back the #handtohandmarket because of onerepweightlifting expanding. 

H2HMRKT : HOLIDAY 2025
12/06-12/07 Sat. & Sun. 10:00-2:00PM
At ONE REP  207 S Westgate Dr STE A

If you’re looking for another opportunity to sling zines and your handmade goods, APPLY 🙌🩷.
Last day to do so is tonight at midnight. 
Link to register in profile! 
Much Love,
TM

events.title: 🎵 Rough Romance Tour
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 17th!
Doors at 6:30pm
Show at 7:30pm

Two internationally touring folk/Americana duos, The Rough& Tumble and Flagship Romance combine for a night of high energy, four part harmonies, infectious hooks and a roomful of laughter. It took over 10 years and 500,000 miles of touring the indie/folk circuit and seeing each other’s posters on venue walls before the two bands met. But it only took one meeting before they’d started a text thread and concocted the idea of the Rough Romance tour. It’s a night of open-armed, deep-in-the-belly-songs that are as intimate as they are universal and as delightful as your first cup of coffee. It’s a jubilant show that has become thehighlight of the year for many people to watch these four accomplished musicians and friends share the stage. 

Flagship Romance is an unforgettable alternative folk duo known for their breathtaking vocal harmonies, inspired songwriting, dynamic live show, and a remarkable on and off-stage chemistry that will leave you with the feeling that you made two new best friends. Married singers-songwriters Shawn Fisher and Jordyn Jackson effortlessly fuse technical precision in their impeccably tight vocal blend with an invigorating sense of emotional abandon in their energetic live persona. Performing each show as if it could be their last, this duo will have you questioning how their larger-than-life sound can come from just two voices and an acoustic guitar.

The Rough & Tumble are a hardscrabble folk/Americana duo, made up of Mallory Graham and Scott Tyler. With accordions and guitars, close-harmonies, ukuleles and oddball percussion, their commanding stage presence and engaging performances have made them a not-to-miss band when they tour through town because you won’t see the same show twice. They have been embraced by fans for their stark honesty, unique storytelling and well-crafted melodies especially after 2024’s release, Hymns For My Atheist Sister and Her Friends To Sing Along To, a collection of deeply personal, unifying and uplifting, humanist gospel songs.

events.title: 🎵 Jonathan Live DJ Set
events.description:
Tonight, Saturday the 16th!
9pm - 2am

Jonathan is an electronic music artist crafting refined, high-energy house experiences rooted in rhythm, culture, and flow. Known for his elegant mixing style and ability to build momentum with intention, his sets move seamlessly across House, Afro House, and Deep sounds, creating immersive journeys on the dance floor.

As a resident of Ibiza Stardust Radio, Jonathan delivers a monthly one-hour showcase of global house music, connecting his sound with an international audience. His musical identity blends European and Latin influences with a modern electronic edge, shaping a style that is both dynamic and deeply curated.

He also serves as the official DJ for Noches Latinas in Greensboro, a long-running cultural event that brings together over 1,000 attendees per night across diverse communities. While this platform highlights his versatility, his core focus remains rooted in house music, where his sound, energy, and artistic direction fully align.

Filling out the night we have our resident DJs keeping holding it steady. As always, help us spread the word and we will see you Saturday!

events.title: 🎵 Dance From Above
events.description: Unfortunately today’s matinee show has been cancelled! See you tonight for Dance From Above!

events.title: 🎵 Candi Jenkins & Blue Cactus
events.description:
Tonight, Friday the 15th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Candi Jenkins blends country and folk songs shaped by truth, grit, and the courage to start over. Her project began after canceling her wedding on the day of the ceremony—a moment that inspired her cornerstone song “Wild Blue.” The experience sparked a new chapter of healing and courage to speak the truth. 
Blending classic country warmth with cinematic western imagery, Candi creates music that feels intimate, emotional, and deeply human. She has opened for Clay Street Unit and Jason Boland & The Stragglers, with upcoming performances supporting Son Little and Andy Frasco.
Her voice is raw, her stories are real, and her art is rooted in the belief that transformation is always possible.

Rooted in Chapel Hill, North Carolina, Blue Cactus got their start among the active honky-tonk circuit, developing an electrified twang that now branches out effortlessly into folk, alt-country, and psychedelic-tinged rock. Having released three records to critical acclaim, they have grown a cult following in the South and beyond, their introspective lyricism and blistering guitar techniques making the band potent in the studio and on stage.  Their 2025 album Believer was hailed in Bandcamp’s Best New Country list and earned them slots on SXSW, Hopscotch Fest’s main stage opening for Geese and My Morning Jacket, and PBS’ new original series, Shaped By Sound. Blue Cactus have toured throughout the US, developing a dedicated cult following. From intimate clubs to major festival stages, Blue Cactus is known for connecting deeply with audiences wherever they go.

events.title: 🎵 The Turkey Buzzards & Stripmall Ballads
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 14th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

“Much like the duo themselves, the songs range near and far, from the sticky humidity of the Carolinas to the arid and dusty cellars of the West, telling stories of simplistic beauty that unravel through gritty vocals and thoughtful harmonies. The bare authenticity of each song sweeps up the listener for a ride that might wander but will certainly not end in the same place where it began.” 

Dylan Nicholson (Greensboro, NC) and Eric Patterson (Lodi, CA) formed the The Turkey Buzzards in Morro Bay, CA and have shared stages with John R. Miller, Robert Earl Keen, Jeffery Martin, The Deslondes, Dawes, White Buffalo, Brothers Comatose, and frequently join Willy Tea Taylor as members of The Fellership.

Stripmall Ballads is the starkly poetic project of Phillips Saylor Wisor, and Everybody Wins is his most fully realized statement yet—a raw, cinematic folk record mapping the forgotten corners of America with grit and grace. Produced by J. Tom Hnatow and recorded at Small Blue Studio in Lexington, Kentucky, the album features John R. Miller and The Engine Lights, with guest appearances from Lydia Loveless, Willy Tea Taylor, and Bob Sumner. Drawing comparisons to Neil Young, Bonnie “Prince” Billy, and the lineage of Townes Van Zandt, Gillian Welch, and Jason Molina, Wisor writes slow-burning ballads set in flood zones, mill towns, and long-empty bedrooms, tracing survival, loss, and fleeting connection on the margins. With a lived-in voice and piercing lyrical empathy, Stripmall Ballads delivers songs that honor overlooked lives—and hit with the kind of emotional weight that resonates deeply on stages and festival grounds.

events.title: 🎵 Kevn Kinney
events.description:
Thursday, May 28th, 2026!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Kevn Kinney is most widely known as founder and frontman of Atlanta-based rock band DrivinN-Cryin. A highly-acclaimed singer, songwriter, musician, performer, visual artist and poet, he 
has recently been inducted into both the Wisconsin Music Hall of Fame and Georgia Music Hall 
of Fame for his impactful legacy of art.
Kinney has released multiple critically acclaimed solo and band recordings during his career 
having also collaborated with Warren Haynes, REM, The Who and John Popper, among others. 
Peter Buck of REM has produced and performed on several of Kevn’s albums and continues to 
be involved in his career.
Kevn Kinney has continuously toured full-time with his band Drivin n Cryin while simultaneously 
carving out a niche for himself as a solo artist. From the festival stage to the smallest house 
concert, fans are always excited at the opportunity to see him up close and personal in a 
stripped-down acoustic setting. 
Recent touring include multiple dates with the Indigo Girls, Blackberry Smoke, The Black 
Crowes and others.
His latest solo studio album Think About It was released in 2022. In Fall 2023 the first release of 
the Let’s Go Dancin’: A Compilation Celebrating The Songs Of Kevn Kinney four-album vinyl 
series came out with over 100 digital recorded titles slated to go through end of 2024. The 
project is a massive all-star tribute to songwriter, poet, performer Kevn Kinney. Tracks from 
Jason Isbell, Violent Femmes, Shovels & Rope, Mike Farris, Great Peacock and dozens more 
will be released.

events.title: 🎵 Desirée Dawson & Mira Lazuli
events.description:
Tomorrow, Wednesday the 13th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Desirée Dawson stands out as a multi-talented artist, celebrated for her remarkable achievements as a recording artist, songwriter, producer, music therapy facilitator, and yoga teacher. Her main instrument is her powerful yet soothing voice which is often accompanied with her Baritone Ukulele. Notably, her outstanding musical journey includes a prestigious JUNO Award nomination, recognizing her album “Meet You At The Light.” She has recent viral success thanks to her new songs Cup Of Tea and I Am Blessed. Over the last few years she has been nominated for a Juno award, won best music video at SXSW and was nominated for an UK music video award. Whether performing solo or in harmony with her band, Desirée’s sentiment of care and compassion will lovingly stay with you and be a gift to pass along.

Mira Lazuli is an Algerian-Quebecois folk artist based in New Orleans. They write songs about queer relationships, healing and self love in a style that mixes dark folk, old-time Appalachian and indie folk.

events.title: 🎵 Bee Million w/ Panes and Burlap Circus
events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 7th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Durham, NC, songwriter Ben Newgard formed Bee Million with his brother, drummer Anders Newgard, and best friend and lead guitar player David Smith, before adding bass extraordinaire Lucas Tax to round out the roster. With sonic influences spanning the likes of everything from Angel Olsen to the Rolling Stones to Guided by Voices (after whom they’re named), Bee Million weaves together a rough-and-ragged yet melodic and infectious strand of rock n roll that will linger with you long after the music’s ended.

W/ Panes and Burlap Circus

events.title: 🎵 Joe Hooten & Jade Moore
events.description:
Tonight, Wednesday the 6th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Asheville-based singer-songwriter Joe Hooten, originally from Macon, Georgia, leads Tin Roof Echo—a project rooted in introspective songwriting, melodic grit, and a DIY spirit. Since his 2014 debut The Original Plan, he’s released seven albums blending indie rock and alt-country with a raw, lived-in feel.

His latest release, Flowers Falling, was recorded with producer Kevin Boggs and mastered by John Keane (R.E.M., Indigo Girls), featuring members of Steep Canyon Rangers and Drive-By Truckers. The result is a warm, country-leaning record with understated punch.

Hooten has played everything from festivals like Enofest and Finsterfest to venues like the Grey Eagle and the Orange Peel, sharing bills with Scott Miller, Kenny Roby, Freedy Johnston, David Childers, Pylon Reenactment Society, Agent Orange, and Soul Asylum.

Jade Moore is a powerhouse singer-songwriter from the Charlotte area whose music carries the pulse of Appalachian rhythm and roots straight to the soul. Drawing deep from the soil of the Carolinas, her sound is shaped by blues, jazz, bluegrass, and Americana, braided together with stories pulled from her own past and the wider universe that surrounds her songwriting.

With over a decade of full-time performance experience, Jade commands the stage with a voice that feels both timeworn and electric. There’s a fire in her delivery and a reverence for tradition in her phrasing. Each song ignites something familiar, a reminder of front-porch harmonies, winding back roads, and the resilient spirit of the region she calls home.

Her live performances are immersive and dynamic, blending genre-spanning favorites with striking original material that is rich in emotion, raw honesty, and rhythmic drive. Whether she’s leaning into deep Southern soul, slipping into sultry jazz tones, or delivering heartfelt Americana ballads, Jade creates moments that linger long after the last note fades.

Rooted in Appalachia yet reaching far beyond it, Jade Moore’s music sparks connection, stirs memory, and invites listeners to feel the rhythm of the land, the story of the song, and the truth in every lyric.

In ancient Polynesia, when a terrible curse incurred by the demigod Māui reaches Moana's island, she answers the Ocean's call to seek out Māui to set things right.

events.title: 🤝 Author Spotlight: Leigh Ann Henion & C.T. Finney
events.description:
✨ Author Spotlight! ✨

New York Times best-selling author Leigh Ann Henion, will share insights from her book, "Night Magic: Adventures Among Glowworms, Moon Gardens, and Other Marvels of the Dark". Known for her immersive explorations of the natural world, Henion invites readers to embrace the beauty and mystery of nature after dark. 🌙

Henion and environmental educator C.T. Finney will discuss the power of darkness in the lives of so many animals, and the deep connections between storytelling and environmental awareness. After the discussion, participants can take part in a guided outdoor activity.

ℹ Email melanie.buckingham@greensboro-nc.gov or call 336-373-2923 to register.

events.title: 🤝 Dr. Susan J. Eischeid: The Women's Orchestra at Auschwitz-Birkenau
events.description:
The women’s orchestra at Auschwitz-Birkenau, composed entirely of prisoners, was unique in the Nazi camp system. Dr. Susan J. Eischeid, who spent decades researching the orchestra and interviewing survivors, will present a history of the ensemble and dispel some of the myths surrounding it. She will also discuss conductor and virtuoso violinist, Alma Rosé, and founder, Maria Mandl, Head Overseer of the women's camp and one of the worst perpetrators of the Holocaust.

Dr. Susan J. Eischeid is an author, musician, and teacher, specializing in the music of the Holocaust. Professor Emeritus of Oboe at Valdosta State University, she has spent two decades researching the women’s orchestra at Auschwitz-Birkenau. She is the author of "Mistress of Life and Death: The Dark Journey of Maria Mandl", "Head Overseer of the Women’s Camp at Auschwitz-Birkenau", and "The Truth about Fania Fénelon and the Women's Orchestra of Auschwitz-Birkenau".

📍 Central Library
📆 Sunday, May 17
⏰ 3 - 4:30 pm

events.description:
Our AAPI Heritage Month Cinema series continues with a screening of "Mulan", the original Disney musical starring Ming-Na Wen based on the Chinese legend of Hua Mulan.

When Imperial China calls one man from every family to defend the empire from invading Huns, a young woman disguises herself as a soldier to take her ailing father’s place. events.title: 🤝 End-of-Year Raffle
events.description:
🎁 End-of-Year Raffle Time!
We wanted to thank you for showing up and reading with us.
✨ At our next meeting — Sunday, Dec 7 at 12pm at scuppernongbooks — we’ll be hosting a raffle with three prize bundles.
Each prize includes:

📚 a Blind Date With a Book
🌿 + bookish goodies from local small businesses we love (boroughcoffeegso chandlersdtgreensboro thetinygreenhouse scuppernongbooks )

Here’s how to enter:
✨ If you’ve attended 6 or more meetings this year, just give us your name at our Sunday meet up.

✨ Can’t make it this weekend? Send us a DM to let us know you’ve hit 6 meetings, and we’ll add you to the raffle.

If you’re just now joining us, we’d still like to see you on Sunday! 

✨ Thanks for reading with us.
.
.
.

events.title: 🛍️ Market Event
events.description:
Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays 🎄 As we approach the end of the year , we are thankful for all of the love and support we’ve gotten thus far. It’s never easy starting something new, you’ve gotta just put your best foot forward and go. But with God, the right people around you and a community-centric town like Greensboro, there’s no way to lose ❤️ so I appreciate everyone who has shared a post, told a friend, or dragged a family member to one of our events. We’ve done a lot in 9 months thanks to y’all, looking forward to what the next year brings! 

And since it’s the giving season we figured we’d give you the date of our next event 😄 February 1st! More details and vendor information coming soon . Stay Tuned, Stay Warm, Stay Safe! 🫡

📸 | shottbyverde

events.title: 🤝 Release and Return Flight of Cele Fierro
events.description:
"On the morning of Tuesday, October 7, the Argentine Embassy in Tel Aviv reported that Israeli authorities would transfer our comrade Cele Fierro along with other activists from Ketziot prison to the Jordanian border for release.

Our comrade Cele, who was aboard the Adara, along with two other Argentines, will fly out of Jordan early in the morning of Wednesday, October 8 (local time), to arrive in Buenos Aires later that same night.

We are proud of Cele’s internationalist militancy, putting her body on the line in the risky mission of the GSF, representing the MST of Argentina and the International Socialist League (ISL)." ligainternacionalsocialista cele_fierro
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Trial of Alejandro Bodart
events.description:
Palestine solidarity is not a crime, solidarity with Alejandro Bodart. At the end of this month Argentine socialist, Alejandro Bodart will be on trial once again for his solidarity and support of the Palestinian people and their resistance to genocide. Bodart’s trial is not only about Bodart, it is an international trend to criminalize any expression of solidarity with Palestine, while the State of Israel has murdered over 180,000 Palestinians. Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists stand in solidarity with Bordart and all those across the world resisting genocide. #absolucionparabodart
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Coalition to Reject A MAGA America Panel & Organization Fair
events.description:
Come join the Coalition to Reject A MAGA America for a panel, community discussion and organization fair. Speakers from different local and international struggles will be speaking about their campaigns and organizational efforts to give local activists spaces where they can get involved in the struggle. A members Third Act will be speaking about how to stop the Transco SSEP pipeline, workers at REI and Reconsidered goods will be speaking on their unionization efforts, international speakers will be speaking on the struggles in Syria and Bangladesh, and activists in the October 22nd coalition will be speaking about the fight to end police violence. After the panel will be a community discussion followed by an organization fair where various different local orgs will have tables set up for participants to engage with. 
.
#organizethesouth #resist #wekeepussafe
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🤝 Special Meeting: Borders are prisons and prisons are borders
events.description:
Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists would like to invite you to join us this Monday, February 10 at 7pm at 1333 Grove St for a special meeting, featuring a talk by organizer Kristen Godfrey on their article, "Borders are prisons and prisons are borders" with a discussion to follow.

*

"The way working-class people experience borders all over the world is tied to the theft of land and surplus value. Settler-colonialism’s life source is criminality and illegality, which is why we must be anti-capitalist abolitionists, developing a critical analysis of how imperialists’ tactics work to divide us."

*

Tell us how this new context impacts you. Let's think about how we can resist together.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: 🎵 “Spotlight” Launch Party
events.description:
Latour & Jimmy D pres. “Spotlight” Launch Party tonight at elsewhere with an extended 3.5hr b2b. Support from Rav & Cobella

Golden Hour 9-10

events.title: 🎵 WAXX OFF Debut
events.description:
Welcoming the debut to elsewhere of Australia’s homegrown UKG star waxxoffmusic this July. Tickets on sale in bio now

WAXX OFF is a frontline dj and producer from adelaide, south australia, shaping australia’s current wave of underground uk garage and speed garage. renowned for high-impact, high energy performances, his sets command the dance-floor through purposeful, momentum-driven programming. his productions have gained global traction through releases on influential labels including ec2a, sweat it out and no instruction, earning support from key figures such as interplanetary criminal, malugi, marlon hoffstadt and dr dubplate just to name just a few.following a year of relentless touring throughout australia, new zealand and asia in 2025; including standout performances at a3 festival, beyond the valley and wildlands, waxx off has firmly established himself as one of the country’s most exciting producers and selectors.beyond his solo output, his work with ukg outfit inside kru has delivered beatport top 100 charting records and multiple national sellouts, further solidifying his position within the scene entering 2026 with a wave of new music and clear momentum, waxx off is positioned for his biggest year to date

events.title: 🎵 Spotlight Launch Party
events.description:
This Saturday we host the launch party of the new joint record from elsewhere’s two music directors Latour + Jimmy D, who’ve teamed up to release “Spotlight” on the iconic Central Station Records. 

Latour b2b Jimmy D for a 3.5hr set this Saturday
with support from Rav & Cobella. 

About the record:
Latour and Jimmy D are two music directors on the Gold Coast at elsewhere who’ve come together to write Spotlight. (their second collab)

Jimmy’s history dates back to being a vinyl DJ on the Sydney circuit in the 90’s, we wanted to bring back the flavour of that era with new updated NRG, that holds weight on today’s systems and events. 

The record being a standout memorable tune of the night with its stellar vocal which sees the majority of the record stripped away leaving a sassy ear catching moment in the rave. Then slowly creeping back up to an all our NRG finale. Think donk basslines, 90s rave synths & fast grooving raw drums.

A record to make SURE the dancefloor is indeed dancing. And if you aren’t then, “getttt the f offfffff “

events.title: 🎵 blake.08
events.description:
This Saturday we welcome back anjunadeep artist blake.08_  no online tickets, door only from 9pm

blake.08 stands out as a rare breed of electronic music producer – one who balances underground credibility with broad appeal, and who has earned respect from dance music’s veterans while staying firmly grounded in his own vision. from humble beginnings in a sydney bedroom studio to gracing international stages, blake gilray’s journey is a testament to patience, authenticity, and passion for the art. with a global cult following behind him, support from industry heavyweights, and a debut album on the way, 2025 is poised to be the year blake.08 cements his place among electronic music’s exciting new leaders. whether he’s dropping a late-night club heater or a blissful downtempo anthem, blake.08 does it on his own terms – and the world is listening

events.title: 🤝 EICC Book Drive
events.description:
Throughout the month of April, Scuppernong will have a box at our register for this book drive for the youth program at Eastside Immigrant Community Center, a part of the Center for New North Carolinians at UNCG. cnnc.uncg ⁠
⁠
We're collecting grade school to YA books, with a comprehensive wishlist and guidelines at the EICC Book Drive link in our bio. Feel free to stop by the store to drop books in the box, or buy some on our website and leave us a note that it's for the drive! ⁠
⁠
📚️💞⁠
⁠
#bookdrive #ScuppernongBooks #downtownGSO #greensboro

events.title: 🤝 Trans Rights Readathon
events.description:
Come grab a book from us to get a head start on the Trans Rights Readathon! This year it runs from March 17th-31st, with the 31st being Trans Day of Visibility. Find more details and ways to get involved at transrightsreadathon 

💙💟💗

#trr #transrightsreadathon #tdov

events.title: 🤝 Downtown Greenway Grand Opening

Produced by Creative Greensboro, CultureWorks is a free, day-long festival shining a spotlight on the city’s artists, designers, and creatives. Taking place at the Greensboro Cultural Center and LeBauer Park, 11am–8pm.

events.description:
Downtown Greensboro has a PACKED weekend ahead. Here are just 4 of the MANY events happening this weekend! Keep an eye out for our Weekend Lineup tomorrow! 

The TowneBank Summer Concert Series kicks off tomorrow, April 30th with Chairman of the Board. Tickets available on our website or at the gsohoppers box office!

First Saturday Stroll on Elm is back with vendors, live entertainment, and Free Comic Book Day with Strange Ways Comics. 

💚The first 50 people to bring a receipt to the DGI tent from aSaturday purchase with a Downtown business will receive a free tote bag and a $5 Boro Buck gift card! 

The Downtown Greenway launches Green May with Wheels on the Greenway this Saturday!

Greensboro Food Truck Festival rolls in this Sunday!

Send this to your friends and make your plans!

events.title: 🌈 Heidi N Closet at Chemistry
events.description:
⚠️ ATTENTION! ⚠️

In the interest of public safety and overcrowding concerns, we’ve had to make some difficult decisions regarding Heidi N Closet’s return to the Chemistry stage.

We truly appreciate all the excitement surrounding this event, and we know Heidi appreciates the incredible support from her hometown crowd as well! However, to ensure everyone’s safety and to remain within venue occupancy limits, the following changes will be in effect:

💲 Cover Charges (21+ Only)
• FREE before 9 PM
• $10 from 9:00 PM – 11:59 PM
• $20 from Midnight until Close

🚫 This event is strictly 21+
Guests ages 18–20 will not be permitted entry.

Thank you for understanding and helping us keep this event safe and enjoyable for everyone. We can’t wait to welcome our girl home and party with you!
 Greensboro's LGBTQ+ nightclub

events.title: 🌈 Pride Celebration
events.description:
Are you ready to celebrate Pride?!? Pride in our gender identity, gender expression, and sexual orientation happens year-round, and it's also great to have opportunities to celebrate together!

Join us at the Pyrle Theater in downtown Greensboro for a day of celebration on Saturday, June 20. The fun kicks off in the afternoon with family-friendly songs led by Mr. Sam of Scuppernong's Saturday Get-Downs, followed by a pizza dance party for youth and teens. To close out the night, the 21+ crowd will be treated to performances by drag superstars Thigh Fieri, Franny Starlight, and Brenda the Drag Queen, with DJ SmoothE providing tunes throughout the evening.

The Pyrle will have food and drinks available to keep the fun going. Tickets will be available soon through the Pyrle, so stay tuned!
 Advancing equality and inclusion through philanthropy, programming, and advocacy that uplifts the LGBTQ community.

Join us for the KICKOFF of "First Tuesdays with Friends" TONIGHT, Tuesday April 7.  This is a new monthly social presented by GGF and Lewis & Elm that will be held on the first Tuesday of each month. Come enjoy some wine, cocktails, tasty non-alcoholic options, and delicious food in downtown Greensboro's Southend.
Lewis & Elm will be donating 15% of cocktail and mocktail sales to promote the vision of GGF to create a thriving LGBTQ community in the Triad.
This new monthly event will be in addition-to our Takeovers, where we will hop around the Triad.

events.title: 🍽️ Community Art Grant Application Deadline
events.description:
14 MORE DAYS TO APPLY!!!! We are grateful to be in community and partnership with East White Oak Community Center and Creative Greensboro! Link to Apply in our bios! 

Text forwarded from creativegreensboro Creative Greensboro has awarded its second Community Art grant to local organization, gso_mutual_aid! They are currently looking for artists to apply for a variety of public led art projects in the East White Oak community, including:

An indoor mural at Peeler Recreation Center that showcases the history of East White Oak Community Center.

Educational/way-finding signage and a Little Free Library onsite at East White Oak Community Center.

Artwork on the basketball court backboards at nearby Bywoods Park

If you are an interested Guilford County based artist apply online by March 31 at 5 pm. Link is in our bio!

events.description:

events.description:
Unleash your inner writer at the 2026 Greensboro Bound Book Festival.

On April 11, ericamiriamfabri joins our o.henrymag Writers Workshops, leading Making the Public Personal: Writing Autobiographical Poetry Inspired by Current Events.

Author of “Dialect of a Skirt” and “Morphology,” Fabri brings a powerful perspective on writing from lived experience.

events.title: 🤝 2026 Greensboro Bound Book Festival
events.description:
libbybuckwrites brings her debut novel “Port Anna” to the 2026 Greensboro Bound Book Festival on April 11.

Set along the rugged coast of Maine, the novel explores family, memory, and the pull of place, revealing how personal histories and changing communities shape a broader American story.
